scenario: A junior operator at Beacon Bank has just fixed the orderValue variable on a stalled Camunda 8.8 instance. The instance is 1 of 40 incidents the team needs to clear this morning. The operator now needs to actually re-run the failed step so the instance can progress to the next activity in the model.
question: What action on the instance page actually moves the process past the resolved incident?

options.text: Initiate a retry of the process instance using the retry control in the top right corner of the page or the one below the Operations heading
Save the edited variable; Operate detects the change and resumes the instance automatically without any retry button being pressed by the operator
Cancel the instance and start a fresh one with the corrected variable values from the process definition list view in Operate
Wait for the broker's automatic retry interval to elapse so the engine re-evaluates the failed gateway condition with the new variable value

a.text: Correct. After editing the variable, the operator must initiate a retry. The instance page exposes the retry control in the top right corner and again below the Operations heading; either one triggers the re-evaluation.

b.text: Incorrect. Saving the variable change does not automatically resume the instance; the operator must explicitly initiate a retry to re-evaluate the failed step.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented flow keeps the original instance and retries it after editing the variable, rather than cancelling and recreating, which would lose context and instance history.

d.text: Incorrect. Resolving an incident in Operate requires an explicit operator-initiated retry; there is no automatic broker retry that picks up edited variables after a condition-evaluation incident.

explanation: Editing a variable resolves the data problem, but the engine still needs to be told to re-run the failed step. Operate exposes two retry controls on the process instance page: one in the top right corner and one below the Operations heading; either one initiates the retry.
